This app is good for multiple age groups, using a lower piece count for beginners. Even the price of add-on packs is extremely reasonable. The variety of free packs is impressive. A Win-Win app.

Review 1: rating 4.0. Just what I need This app is exactly what I've been looking for. I have a big collection of digital comics and this allows me to read them on my kindle without the app itself taking up too much space. I use comic rack on my desktop, but sometimes I just want to get my comic on while lounging or on the go.The interface is easy to use a nice looking enough. I love that I can have folders for each comic collection I'm reading. So far, I haven't had any issues. I definitely rec this one. Sets the standard for ALL Comic Reader apps! 😀 Single handedly the BEST comic reader. I can't praise enough; if you have comics anywhere on your device, this app will find it and organize it for you. wanna transfer from you're computer to the app? This app make it as easy as possible. There is a plethora of options within this app to make it the best possible experience for reading your comics whether it's a foreign comic or a western comic, you can choose how the comic will read, wh... I paid $2.99, and now at $1.49, it's even better. 5 stars!! Review 5: rating 4.0. Awesome comic reading app This is a fantastic comic book app. reading comics in CBR format, and arranges books the way you'd see them on the racks of a comic book shop. If I have a complaint, it's in the lack of a 2 page viewing option. If the comic is scanned with a 2 page to 1 page ratio, it's great, but i'd like the option to see pages side by side, even if it wasn't a 2 page spread to begin with. Good color appearance, reacts well, and I would recommend this for folks who dig rea... Review 6: rating 5.0. Amazing comic book viewer This app works great for me. It automatically detects my cbr and cbz files. It's easy to organize magazines and comics. It shows which ones you've read and automatically opens to the page you left off even after a few days. There are many settings to optimize your reading experience the way you want. If you have a lot of comics, this app is so worth it.
Thanks to the developers for this great app. It completes my kindle. Review 7: rating 5.0. Great app for the Kindle Fire, really! Review 1: rating 5.0. A great game It's kind of sad how this game is attracting a bunch of negative reviews through no fault of it's own. I installed it on my Fire TV and it worked perfectly. It even has support for the Fire TV remote which is rare for a platformer like this.

If you're having trouble installing a game or are disappointed your device isn't supported, try emailing Amazon or Disney about it. Review 2: rating 5.0. Fantastic Do you remember the game Castle of Illusion for the Sega Genesis? Well, it happened in 1990 and you can be forgiven for forgetting about it. My brother and I rented it from a local video store and played the crap out of it. Anyway, what we have here is a clever re-imagining of that Genesis game.

Visually, the game is beautiful and fluid. You can see elements of the original game peppered throughout the levels. And the levels are no walk in the park. You will prob... Review 3: rating 2.0. Review 7: rating 3.0. Great game; frustrating controls Castle of Illusion is a five star game, but this touchscreen port only merits three. Most platformers on touch devices suffer from poor controls and this game is no exception. You will get better at controlling Mickey as you progress, but you'll find yourself experiencing control problems no matter how much time you put in. Edit: If you have a compatible controller, it'd be a good idea to use it. Review 8: rating 4.0. A great revisit! ES File Explorer ES File Explorer is the android version (sort of) Windows Explorer on your PC. It gives you access to the files on your phone or Kindle Fire so that you can manipulate them directly (carefully). This comes in handy for deleting, copying, or moving files, but you do need to make sure you know what you are doing because on a phone or Kindle it is not always as straightforward as it can be on a PC. reference_output: rating 3.0. tricky I do enjoy this game but what frustrates me is how it is controlled so that you will not beat some of the more difficult levels too fast. I have had several games where I had to wait on a specific color of candy with 20 moves left and could not get that particular candy.  I understand that In order for this game to be fun, there needs to be challenges but in my opinion it comes down to the creators wanting you, the player, to spend real money.  In all of the games I have played like this I have never used my hard-earned cash. There are  a few reasons for this, one being that at any time my Kindle can crash.(yes, this has happened TWICE and I am on my 2nd replacement from Amazon) To have any amount of money tied up in something that has the possibility of conking out on me and going thru the hassle of getting it replaced is not worth my time and effort.  I can say this, if I feel that this game is getting too aggravating for the amount of time I put into it, I will remove the game from my device.  My husband would be thrilled if I did!!!

persona: This customer values long-term brand vision and innovation, prioritizing a forward-thinking platform over legacy features, and is willing to support a service they see as industry-leading, even if it means sacrificing past conveniences.
reference_output: rating 5.0. King of online media, no doubt about it What can anyone say about Netflix?  I wish I had purchase a boatload of stock when it was struggling, but such regrets are common when we use that wistful thing called 'hindsight' in our reflections, right?  Netflix is the undisputed champ of online media, and though I was a little more than a little upset when they decided to drop their "real dvd" product loan part of the biz, now I see what the great vision was all about.  They are producing top-notch programs of their own in abundance, and have myriad of movies and series available instantly, and with nary a studder or a shudder in the transmission of their products.  They even allow downloads for much of the media for viewing at a later time, like we did so our grandchildren could watch movies on our 16" convertible laptop during a 9 hour trip up north not long ago. Using headphones, it was the "peacemaker" of all time.

reference_output: rating 4.0. Grandkids love it It is pretty simple.  The kids (2 and 3) learn the mechanics quickly.  They especially like the puzzle games.  That feature culd use more puzzles as they run through them quickly, but they ask for it at every visit, and if allowed, will play it repeatedly.  The rest are good learning tools.  The instructions from the 'moderator' are clear, and assuming they want to succeed, rather than just push buttons, the can follow it.Like any game, there is a tendency for the kids to just push buttons repeatedly regardless of the outcome.  I would recommend the developer add some sort of additional feature to encourage a try of a different option.

Review 1: rating 5.0. Relief I've been using this app in the office for some relief from the oppressing silence we feel in a locked, windowless room. My workmate enjoyed it enough to ask me what it was so that she could download it as well. We love the variety of sounds we can get, and even more since you have the ability to mix the sounds in layers as you like. Handy when music with words or a beat are just too much and wreck your concentration, this is just right. Review 2: rating 5.0. Perfect for Naptime & Overnight Trips I'm one of those people who cant sleep without noise. When I'm at home it's usually a box fan or the television. With this app, I don't feel super uncomfortable when I go on overnight trips. It makes that eery quiet hotel room much more bearable to say the least. It also helps when I'm putting my kids down for an afternoon nap on the weekend. It used to be impossible to get them to settle down enough to take a nap. But now I just put this on,... Review 3: rating 5.0. Free version is excellent! This app is a white noise soundboard so you can customize for whatever mood/preference you have (up to ten noises simultaneously). There are a few mixes under "favorites" or you can add your own. Most importantly, there's a timer (with the option of closing the app at the end) which is great if you're using this to fall asleep to or time a study or yoga session. I'm impressed with the free version, and have tried other white noise apps that just see... Review 4: rating 5.0. I love this app! This app puts me to sleep in just minutes every night. I refused to pay for more chances. Then I discovered there are "cheats" for every level. I even resorted to that, but could not pass. If they built in a feature where if you fail a level 40 times you can go try the next I would play again, but trust me 241 times trying to pass the same stupid game will push anyone to the limit. They have lost me, but have many followers, so try it for yourself.

reference_output: rating 3.0. Hidden Objects - Blackstone Mysteries is fun and challenging Hidden Objects - Blackstone Mysteries is fun and challenging, but frustrating!  The game locks up a lot. reference_output:
rating 4.0. I LOVE THIS APP, have tried them all!!! I started with the free version, I love it so much that I got the pd version then, deleted all the other notepad, word document, spreadsheet, etc apps that I had installed on my Kindle (which was A LOT).
I will change my rating to a 5, if I can get this issue fixed (actually 2 but, just 1 that I have a hard time with). I would have emailed consumer service but, it keep taking me to the user guide for my Kindle Fire.
When I went to the pd version, I thought it would fix the issue but, it hasn't. When I'm online or offline on email, etc. If I want to send a copy, which should be very easy. On the Kindle, you click the menu and it shows to "share" to many apps. My printer, email AND, it shows Office Suite Pro. When I click to send it there, it says "Wi-Fi settings are disabled".
YET, if I open straight into the app and send something to my printer, it goes straight through. It ALSO shows my printer listed as a device on the app, the printer is ALSO wireless from my Wi-Fi??? WEIRD....So, I actually had to reinstall "colornote", I copy my things to it then, have to copy and paste it to my Office Suite. Please help, this is WAY too much trouble for such a great app. I printed out the manual for it but, didn't find an answer to the problem.
It did the same way before I bought the Pro, like I said I thought buying it would fix it.
Another issue, after I bought the Pro I uninstalled the free version. It does NOT show in my installed apps YET, the pic icon I can not remove from the carousel or any other place that it shows.
THIS problem, I can live with but, the first one is time consuming and NEEDLESS with such a great app.
Please tell me how to fix this so, I CAN give a 5. I use and like the app so much, I really want to give it a 5.
Thank you!!
cindyarose@gmail.com

reference_output:
rating 4.0. decently cute! This is another cute and very adorable app. Pretty much a step up from the regular, boring weather app by adding the option to dress up, interact with, and give an umbrella to an adorable bunny. Everyday, you can collect coins from your piggy bank so you don't necessarily need to purchase anything in app--yes, there is IAP, so parents (with kindles), I would set your parental controls before letting your kids play with this app. I like how you can have the bunny dance and whatnot.

The graphics are pretty good and the sounds are nice and not overbearing. I do also like that you have a few options to change/add (like F° to C°, humidity, etc). You can also have multiple places added, which you swipe your finger across the screen to change places. There's also a camera button to take a picture of the screen--though I don't use these options in apps, if you do, it's a great addition to the app.

My only downfalls with this app is that I wish there was an actual back button in the app (you have to use, on kindle, your pull out menu and use that back button) and that the app loads slowly when I first start up the app (not a HUGE problem, just a bit of an annoyance if you want just a quick temperature reading). A plus side, when I allowed the app to automatically find my location, it actually did--and almost all apps say I'm in a different city (I live in a small village in Alaska, so I think the apps have problems locating it?). I also didn't have issues with the app saving my locations.

Overall, so far it looks like a great app. I wanna say that $2 is too much, but it really is decent for this app. I think I would recommend this to someone who doesn't have a weather app and needs one.

The ONLY problem with this game is....if you don't have a Facebook account or more money to play the game, it goes stale on you and it can get expensive. It is a good old match-3 game where you combine stones of different colors and eventually activate "totems" that help you complete levels. It's up to you which totems to activate for each level depending on specific goal for that level. So for each level you need to come up with a strategy and then be fast as levels last only for 1 minute. It is an addictive time killer recommended to all fans ...

I have only been able to watch live feed about 4 or five times it seems to never load the full images of what is on QVC I only get a blank white screen showing the QVC logo at the top right corner then a pink column on the left with the search icon and the watch/legal words, but THAT'S IT!!! no program to watch this happens on both of my T.V.'s I am REALLY DISAPPOINTE... reference_output: rating 1.0. Kindle Fire HD 8.9 As ashamed of myself as I am, these developers should feel disgusted with themselves!!!  Normally, I take a reviewer's review with a grain of salt, and, once again, I did, to my chagrin!  I took the chance,downloaded the App and played 5 levels.  I got out of the App with no problem and logged into Facebook......And, here's where the problems (and anxiety) began.  First, my screen froze, then it just randomly started to post what I had just started to write on a totally different person's post and then, THEN, my keyboard turned to Japanese!!! I do not normally write reviews unless I totally love a game or I think someone is way off base with their review; so, please know I am only writing this to forewarn others who think like me....this app is HORRENDOUS...DON'T TRY IT!!! I was fortunate enough to be able to delete it and my Kindle is working fine now; BUT, who's to say about any other device???!!!??  Also, my antivirus apps found nothing wrong with this app; so, apparently, either those apps suck or these are some sneaky developers who do NOT deserve nor should be allowed to have a game for free, let alone a PAID app!!!

reference_output:
rating 5.0. Great game, no evilness Word to Word is a word association puzzle game. You are given two columns of words and you have to match together words that are related; they can be synonyms (hot-warm), antonyms (hot-cold), compound words (hot-dog) or merely related in some way (hot-summer). Higher levels will have multiple possible matches for some words, making the game much trickier. The game comes with 440 puzzles.

This game is a lot of fun and is actually pretty challenging, especially on the later puzzles. It's also one of the more unique games I've seen so far.

The game works great on my Motorola Xoom tablet.

The best part of this app is that there is absolutely no villainy on the part of the developer. No In-App Purchasing. No OpenFeint, Flurry or other data miners. No permissions of any kind! It's also a relatively small app, at 3.3 MB. And it only costs 99 cents! This is the way to write an app!

Review 1: rating 5.0. Best tool in my tool box! It connects everytime, no matter what app I'm using. The info I get from it, about my vehicles is priceless! Engine load, mpg, throttle position, speed, rpms, engine temperature, manifold pressure, ignition timing and even more info than that! Gives a read on all sensors AND - it clears check engine codes. Awesome tool. Fantastic price! Review 2: rating 5.0. Cleared the Codes! Fast connection, Fast diagnostics, fast clearing of my engine codes. Even told me how many engines starts and miles I had gone since the code came on. Review 3: rating 4.0. very nice app I've used it only a few times, but it has some very good features. It not only displays the codes and readiness monitors for emissions testing, but it also shows live data stream and mode 6 for the more advanced users. Overall, great app. Worth every penny. persona: This customer prioritizes affordability and practical functionality, opting for mid-range accessories that deliver basic performance without overspending, and values ease of setup and compatibility with existing devices for specific troubleshooting needs.
reference_output: rating 4.0. Nice, inexpensive auto diagnostic tool. I bought this mostly for use on a Chevrolet diesel pickup truck.  I didn't invest in a high-priced blutooth module, just a moderate one, but it sync'd up easily with this app on my Fire Phone and provides access to a few sensors.  I can't get it to read boost pressure, but the other standard gauges work ok.  I use it mostly as a speedometer for now, as the gauge cluster on the truck is having the well-known issues with the stepper motors.  When weather warms up I'll remove the cluster and send it off for repair.  In the meantime, this works well.

reference_output:
rating 5.0. Always trust Dr. P We love DR Panda games. They're colorful, well made, no in app purchasing, no ads. A clever two year old can manage them, and certainly kids from three up will find them delightful.

This one has three or four different art media to explore, including a potting wheel. Since I'm a potter, my three year old grand daughter just loves to throw pots with DR Panda. The action is surprisingly realistic and includes throwing, altering the form, and painting it. Then the game fires the pot and presents it to the DR Panda gang, who applaud the work.

Also includes drawing, Mr Potatohead clones, and a couple of other games. This one gets a to of use. As soon as Gracie comes in my door, she grabs the Kindle and finds her art school. If you only buy one app for your little ones, this should be the one :)

reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Miraculously useful app! What a wonderful app that works through headphones or openly. Set your sound environment, either block out daily noise when wearing headphones because you can find a "noise" canceling sound, or use it openly to apply a soothing sound to fall asleep by. It even works as an alarm clock upon selecting an appropriate noise to wake by. Universally useful on my Kindle HD. Review 2: rating 5.0. helps me to sleep I love all the sounds, but the cat purring puts me to sleep the quickest. It's a great app for background noise. Review 3: rating 5.0. Just right This is a very well-conceived white noise app with a good variety of choices. My whole family uses this and everyone has their favorite "color". Brown Noise is perfect for me as it most closely resembles the fan I use with this app when it's hot at night. I have several different white noise apps, but I always come back to this one. Very well done and easy to use. Review 4: rating 1.0. Too Bad Great idea. Poor implementation. The sound loops are very short, with the result that some sound like the end of an old record. I tried to download additional sounds. They did not download to the app. Review 5: rating 2.0. not useful as it could be. Needs a setting to start (like most other alarm clock apps) when docked or plugged in. Otherwise too much trouble to bother with. Keep looking. Review 6: rating 2.0. Some Tips. Less motor noise. More of a soft wind sound. I sleep with a fan 365 days a year. I have used Industrial size fans that are less noisy than yours. Also please install a stop switch or an off button.
Sincerely, Jennifer Martin Review 7: rating 5.0. Perfect app for Kindle Fire I am VERY sensitive to noise of all kinds. I initially purchased White Noise Lite (which is free). Although it worked quite well, the free version only runs alone, which means you can't read a Kindle book at the same time you use White Noise Lite. I decided that a couple of bucks was a small price to pay to block out my boyfriend's rock band rehearsal while I read.

There are so many great choices for noises on the White Noise app -- many more th... Review 8: rating 4.0. Good nights rest Great app with many options to find a sound that works for you. Alarm clock is another good feature. Sleeping much better these days. One down fall is that you cannot combine sounds. Review 9: rating 5.0. Wide selection of sounds I'm very happy with White Noise. I have downloaded other apps in the past (years ago) and they were all OK, but for whatever reason I didn't end up using them regularly. I think it's partially because I have more free time now, but in any event, this app is getting regular use and I enjoy picking different sounds for different moods. I'm most partial to Tibetan Bowl, when I really want to let my mind wander. Review 10: rating 5.0. Great for my needs I really enjoy this app and the many different sound options it has. I really like that I can use it on my kindle fire and turn off the screen. It will still run and uses very little battery life even while running for 8 hours. I'm very happy that this was a FAOTD awhile ago or I would have probably never tried it. I would only ask that developers consider putting in a "rain on a metal roof" sound, that's the only one I can think that's missing that my hu... Review 11: rating 5.0. saved my sleep I usually have a manual white noise machine going at night...it blocked out street noise and helped my kids and I sleep. BUT it drives my husband nuts and now that we've moved to a quieter location he really needed a change for his own sleep sanity. Time will tell if this will be a lasting solution, but for the time being it has really helped me. And I LOVE that there is a combination rain and thunder storm option...square! Review 12: rating 4.0. Pretty neat for hearing one background sound to sooth or drown out.. Apps that let me mix sounds bog me down occasionally, so this will help when I'm short on whiles to wile away. (As explained by a tinnitus specialist) . I spent $4,000 on special hearing aids that input white noise onto corrected hearing. Operates by a remote control. I can't wear them to bed, at the quietest time when tinnitus is at its peak w several auditory noises & scrambled voices that are unbearable . As I'm bed ri... Review 15: rating 2.0. This is one of my two favorites. There are several options for sounds from airplanes to frying bacon (ok it really sounds kind of neat).

You have a nice picture to look at while the sound is playing, settings so it can be personalized and a timer and wake up options. Volume of sounds are adjustable.

I highly recommend this one for ease of use, price and qu... Review 18: rating 5.0. Impressive Sound Engine This does a great job of creating ambient noise, which can be really relaxing and helps me relax or even fall asleep. There are over 40 noises, including ocean sounds, running water, and rain (if you can't tell, I like water noises). There is also wind, a clothes dryer, airplane, and tons more. I'm surprised at the selection, first of all, but I'm blown away by how realistic all the noises sound. It is a great way to get some relaxing background noise. The sounds were of good quality and it will let you multi task. Brown noise was my favorite. Review 20: rating 4.0. It does the job. The ads for the free version can get a bit annoyingly repetitive at times but it's not so bad. Personally, I find paying for the upgraded version is HIGHLY worth it, especially if you listen to music a lot.
